About: It is about the fertile, watery climate of the Jiangnan region.
It is about the daily life of the residents of Jiangnan.
It is about a winter landscape.
It is about a group of travelers traversing the Yangtze on their mules.
It is about local fishermen fishing along the banks.
It is about fishing as a leisurely activity.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the shanshui genre.
It is associated with brush and ink landscape painting.
It is associated with the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
It is associated with Emperor Huizong.
It is associated with Jayaatu Khan Tugh Temür.
It is associated with Emperor Wenzong of Yuan.
It is associated with the Southern Tang court.
It is associated with the Song court.
It is associated with the Yuan court.
It is associated with the Ming court.
ConsistsOf: It consists of brush and ink.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 10th century.
CreatedBy: It was created by artist Zhao Gan.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 10th century.
HasA: It has a group of travelers traversing the Yangtze.
It has bold and twisted tree textures.
It has washes and dry strokes to emphasize lighting and volume.
It has flicks of the brush for reeds.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by brush and ink.
It is characterized by mountains and waterscapes.
It is characterized by a winter landscape.
It is characterized by bare trees.
It is characterized by dry reeds.
It is characterized by travelers enduring harsh winds and shivering.
It is characterized by flecks of white pigment illustrating a snowstorm.
It is characterized by local fishermen.
It is characterized by bold and twisted tree textures.
It is characterized by washes and dry strokes.
HeldIn: It was held in the National Palace Museum in Taipei, Taiwan.
It was held in the collections of Chinese imperial families across multiple dynasties.
It was held in the collection of Emperor Huizong.
It was held in the collection of Jayaatu Khan Tugh Temür.
It was held in the Qing court.
IsA: It is a Chinese landscape painting.
It is a prominent example of the shanshui genre.
It is the only extant surviving piece by Zhao Gan.
It is a reminder of his duties to his subjects.
KnownAs: It is known as 江行初雪圖.
It is known as Jiāng xíng chūxuě tú.
KnownFor: It is known for being a prominent example of the shanshui genre.
It is known for being the only extant surviving piece by Zhao Gan.
It is known for its depiction of the fertile, watery climate of the Jiangnan region.
It is known for its depiction of the daily life of the residents of Jiangnan.
It is known for its depiction of a winter landscape.
It is known for its depiction of travelers traversing the Yangtze.
It is known for its depiction of local fishermen.
It is known for the contrast to later landscape paintings which depicts fishing as a more leisurely activity.
It is known for its bold and twisted tree textures.
It is known for evoking the hardships of the everyman.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Southern Tang.
It occurred in the Song dynasty.
It occurred in the Yuan dynasty.
It occurred in the Ming dynasty.
It occurred in the Qing dynasty.
PartOf: It is part of the shanshui genre.
It is part of the collections of Chinese imperial families.
It is part of the National Palace Museum in Taipei, Taiwan.
UsedAs: It is used as a reminder of his duties to his subjects.
UsedIn: It is used in the Xuanhe Huapu.
